theorem: iff_comm (P Q : Prop) (h : P ↔ Q) : Q ↔ P

steps.label: Split the iff into two implications
steps.explanation: Q ↔ P means (Q → P) ∧ (P → Q). The constructor tactic splits it into two subgoals: prove Q → P, then prove P → Q.

steps.label: Forward direction: use the backward part of h
steps.explanation: h.mpr extracts the backward implication Q → P from h : P ↔ Q. This is exactly what we need for the first goal.

steps.label: Backward direction: use the forward part of h
steps.explanation: h.mp extracts the forward implication P → Q from h : P ↔ Q. Second goal done.
